description.text:
<!--
  Thank you for opening up this pull request! Please read this comment carefully
  and make sure to fill in as much info as possible below as well.

  First, the obligatory checklist. You must make sure to follow this list as
  much as possible to make merging your PR as easy as possible:

  1. I documented all behaviour within the code as far as I could.
  2. This PR targets the develop branch and *not* the master branch.
  3. I have tested my changes extensively and paid extra attention to potential
     cross-platform issues (e./g. Cmd/Ctrl/Super key bindings)
  4. I ran the `yarn lint` and `yarn test` commands successfully
  5. I have added an entry to the CHANGELOG.md
  6. I matched my code-style to the repository as far as possible
  7. I agree that my code will be published under the GNUP GPL v3 license
  8. In case I created new files, I added a header to them (see the existing
     files for examples)
  9. Before opening this PR, I ran `git pull` a last time to prevent any merge
     issues

  If you don't know how to fulfill some of the above points, feel free to open
  your PR nevertheless and mention those points where you are unsure. The more
  you can fulfill, the faster we can merge your PR, otherwise it will just take
  longer.
 -->

## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->
This PR implements a debounced word counting plugin to improve typing performance.

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->
The word count computation was moved to a `ViewPlugin` so that it can be debounced using `setTimeout()`. It uses a `StateEffect` to update the existing `countField` value.

Event listeners were added for the new `updateWordCountEffect` so that the UI shows updated counts when they arrive rather than waiting for cursor activity.

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->
Currently, word counts are recomputed every keypress. In larger documents (>50,000 words) this starts to add a small but noticeable, and increasing, delay. By debouncing the counting, typing performance is dramatically improved in large documents. 

Word counts are no longer live, however, they are updated the moment the transaction is dispatched.

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: <!-- OS including version -->

ranked_scenarios.summary: Use LanguageTool API `data` parameter to reduce load; and filter words from local dictionary

description.text:
<!--
  Thank you for opening up this pull request! Please read this comment carefully
  and make sure to fill in as much info as possible below as well.

  First, the obligatory checklist. You must make sure to follow this list as
  much as possible to make merging your PR as easy as possible:

  1. I documented all behaviour within the code as far as I could.
  2. This PR targets the develop branch and *not* the master branch.
  3. I have tested my changes extensively and paid extra attention to potential
     cross-platform issues (e./g. Cmd/Ctrl/Super key bindings)
  4. I ran the `yarn lint` and `yarn test` commands successfully
  5. I have added an entry to the CHANGELOG.md
  6. I matched my code-style to the repository as far as possible
  7. I agree that my code will be published under the GNUP GPL v3 license
  8. In case I created new files, I added a header to them (see the existing
     files for examples)
  9. Before opening this PR, I ran `git pull` a last time to prevent any merge
     issues

  If you don't know how to fulfill some of the above points, feel free to open
  your PR nevertheless and mention those points where you are unsure. The more
  you can fulfill, the faster we can merge your PR, otherwise it will just take
  longer.
 -->

## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->
This PR changes the languagetool API call to pass the document as the `data` parameter, and it filters out languagetool responses which match words in the local user dictionary.

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->
By sanitizing the document into a list of `{ text: string}` and `{ markup: string }` objects, we can pass the JSON to the LanguageTool API, and it will only use the `text` objects to generate errors.

This reduces the load on the API service and increases the number of meaningful lints returned by the API.

The linter now filters out any matches for words that are in the local user dictionary.

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->
~~Currently, it seems that `Task` nodes are not parsed correctly -- in my testing, `[ ]` is marked as a text node. I believe this is because we do not activate [all of the GFM extensions](https://github.com/lezer-parser/markdown?tab=readme-ov-file#user-content-gfm), we only activate the base GFM extension via `markdownLanguage`. There could be other causes, but I have not pinned down the problem.~~ So it actually seems to be an issue with the `parseChildren` method when generating the AST for list items. <-- This should be fixed now.

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: <!-- OS including version --> macOS 26

## Pull Request Checklist

1. **Documentation**: I documented the changes within the code where necessary.
2. **Target Branch**: This PR targets the `develop` branch (not the `master` branch).
3. **Testing**: I changed the readability color scheme and tested the changes on three different monitors for visibility and readability.
4. **Lint and Tests**: I have **not** run `yarn lint` or `yarn test` (please advise if this is necessary).
5. **Changelog Entry**: I have **not** added an entry to `CHANGELOG.md` (please let me know if I should add this).
6. **Code Style**: I matched my code style to the repository as much as possible.
7. **GPL License**: I agree that my code will be published under the GNUP GPL v3 license.
8. **New Files**: No new files were created.
9. **Git Pull**: I ran `git pull` before creating this PR to ensure there are no merge conflicts.

## Description
Updated readability color scheme to reflect the traffic light system (red, yellow, green) for better user comprehension.

## Changes

- Updated the readability color scheme to use a traffic light system: red for poor readability, yellow for average readability, and green for good readability.
- Modified the corresponding CSS classes to reflect the new color scheme.
- Updated comments in the code to explain the new readability logic.
- No breaking API changes were introduced with these modifications.

ranked_scenarios.summary: refactor: Move FSAL parser to utilize the Markdown AST Parser

description.text:
## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->

This PR replaces the FSAL Markdown file parsing logic with our new Markdown AST. This reduces complexity and makes sure that what the file descriptors contain matches what the main editor sees.

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->

* Removed the manual parsing logic from the FSAL file parser
* Utilize the Markdown AST parser to extract the types of nodes that are important for collecting metadata on the files

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->

**Breaking Changes**

- This PR now contains a breaking change: The character count without spaces is now gone. After refactoring, I took the liberty to remove that. The reasons are as follows:
    - I originally implemented that counter because – from a programmer's perspective – a plain character count is meaningful (which is why I included it), but when writing text, what should count is only the amount of non-whitespace characters (which is also the writing target for many teachers and journals)
    - However, since Markdown contains a large amount of non-significant whitespace, or, whitespace that has no bearing on the factual text content of a file (e.g. ending a line with two spaces to signify a single line break), a plain character counter has no real use while writing (academic) texts
    - Thus, **the character counter without spaces is now gone, and the regular character counter now excludes whitespace**
    - This behavior is now unified across the app, so there will only be a single character count, no matter where

There are a few TODOs left:

- [x] I realized that now with the new AST we are able to greatly reduce the complexity of the word counter just as well. So I'll have to go back and do that as well, since it's a no-brainer right now
- [x] Go over the entire code base again and eliminate any remnant of manual parsing of Markdown code
- [x] Remove the old legacy `md2ast` function; also check if there are any other MD parsers left in the code base

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: macOS Ventura 13.3.0

## Description

I figured since @benniekiss is now churning out quite some code, it makes sense for me to also switch to PRs for somewhat bigger changes to avoid issues like the footnote mayhem recently.

This PR refactors the md2html function and will – once it's done – enable us to produce more complete HTML, which primarily includes generating a bibliography for the print preview.

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->

* Refactored the MD2HTML to return a Promise
* Unify option API for the function
* Adapt existing places across the codebase to use this new signature
* TODO: Enable bibliography generation

## Additional information

Tested on: macOS 26 Tahoe
